text: The surgeon begins the parovarian cystectomy by incising the anti-mesenteric border of the broad ligament, aiming to enucleate the entire parovarian cyst intact by cutting in the correct anatomical plane.

The surgeon makes a broad incision in the mesentery of the broad ligament, using gentle traction and counter traction to carefully separate the parovarian cyst from its bed while avoiding damage to underlying blood vessels.
The surgeon applies coagulation at the final point of attachment to facilitate the removal of the parovarian cyst in total from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon ruptures the cyst, allowing the fluid to escape. The cyst is then ribbonized within the peritoneal cavity before being removed and sent for histopathological examination.
